texto: ... every moment of our life has a purpose, that every action of ours, no matter how dull or routine or trivial it may seem in itself, has a dignity and a worth beyond human understanding... For it means that no moment can be wasted, no opportunity missed, since each has a purpose in man’s life, each has a purpose in God’s plan. Think of your day, today or yesterday. Think of the work you did, the people you met, moment by moment. What did it mean to you- and might it have meant for God? Is the question too simple to answer, or are we just afraid to ask it for fear of the answer we must give?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Walter J. Ciszek que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “... every moment of our life has a purpose, that every action of ours, no matter how dull or rout…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre tempo, philosophy, religion, com foco em trabalho e religion.
